title: cephaelis cymosa
intro: Cephaelis cymosa, also known as the "Wild Ipecacuanha" or "South American Ipecac", is a plant species native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its emetic and expectorant effects, which are due to the presence of the glycoside emetine. The health benefits of Cephaelis cymosa include its ability to stimulate coughing and vomiting, making it a potential treatment for respiratory infections, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, Cephaelis cymosa is often used in tinctures, infusions, and ointments, although it should be used with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
